name: jira
description: Fork of @aashari/mcp-server-atlassian-jira: generic Jira REST passthrough (jira_get/post/put/patch/delete) plus jira_attach/jira_get_attachment ported from community PR #173, with a fix for binary attachment/thumbnail download corruption (upstream lossily UTF-8-decoded non-textual response bodies).

use_dotenv.title: Read credentials from a .env file
use_dotenv.description: ON: read Atlassian credentials from the .env file below (default ~/.claude/jira.env), and leave the three fields below empty. OFF: enter them in the fields below instead.

dotenv_path.title: .env file path
dotenv_path.description: Path to a .env file holding ATLASSIAN_SITE_NAME / ATLASSIAN_USER_EMAIL / ATLASSIAN_API_TOKEN. Used only when the toggle above is ON. A leading ~ expands to your home directory.

atlassian_site_name.title: Atlassian site name
atlassian_site_name.description: The subdomain in https://<site>.atlassian.net (e.g. "ufcit"). Leave empty when using the .env file above.

atlassian_user_email.title: Atlassian account email
atlassian_user_email.description: Your Jira login email. Leave empty when using the .env file above.

atlassian_api_token.title: Atlassian API token
atlassian_api_token.description: Generate at id.atlassian.com -> Security -> API tokens. Leave empty when using the .env file above. When set, it is stored by Claude Desktop in the OS keychain (sensitive), never in this repo.

tools.name: jira_get
tools.description: Generic GET request to the Jira REST API (JQ-filterable, TOON/JSON output)

tools.name: jira_post
tools.description: Generic POST request to the Jira REST API

tools.name: jira_put
tools.description: Generic PUT request to the Jira REST API

tools.name: jira_patch
tools.description: Generic PATCH request to the Jira REST API

tools.name: jira_delete
tools.description: Generic DELETE request to the Jira REST API

tools.name: jira_attach
tools.description: Upload a file to a Jira issue as an attachment (multipart/form-data)

tools.name: jira_get_attachment
tools.description: Download a Jira attachment to a local file, byte-exact
